messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following school canteen cashless payment system notice for GDPR compliance:

"The school uses a cashless payment system for canteen purchases. Pupils are assigned a unique ID linked to their canteen account. Parents top up accounts online. Purchase history is visible to parents via the parent portal. Data is retained for the current academic year plus 1 year. We use CaterApp as our canteen management provider."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PARTIAL] Contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) for payment processing and public task (Art. 6(1)(e)) for school administration are implied but not stated.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Food purchase history can reveal dietary requirements linked to religion or health (e.g., halal, gluten-free, diabetic meals). No acknowledgement or safeguard stated.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Canteen payment processing with parental visibility is a clear and specific purpose.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Unique ID and purchase history are proportionate for cashless canteen management.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] Current academic year plus 1 year is clearly stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [FAIL] No data subject or parental rights mentioned.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PARTIAL] CaterApp named as provider but no DPA reference stated.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [FAIL] Not addressed. CaterApp infrastructure may involve non-EEA processing.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed. Schools processing pupil data are required to appoint a DPO as public authorities.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [FAIL] Not addressed.

VERDICT: Non-Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 6 (lawful basis not stated), Art. 9 (dietary inference risk not addressed), Art. 15-21 (no rights), Art. 28 (CaterApp DPA not referenced), Art. 46 (transfer safeguards absent), Art. 37 (school DPO required), Art. 33 (no breach notification)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- State lawful basis for each processing activity
- Acknowledge dietary data may reveal religious or health information and state safeguards
- Add parental rights section including access, rectification, and erasure
- Reference CaterApp DPA and confirm EU/UK data processing
- Address international transfer safeguards if CaterApp processes outside UK/EEA
- Disclose school DPO contact
- Add breach notification commitment
